expectations.text: Tells user to create a GPU session with colab new --gpu
expectations.evidence: Lines 45-49: 'colab new -s train-cifar --gpu T4' under the heading 'Create a Session with GPU'

expectations.text: Tells user to upload script files with colab upload
expectations.evidence: Lines 55-57: 'colab upload -s train-cifar ~/ml/train_cifar.py /content/train_cifar.py' under the heading 'Upload the Training Script'

expectations.text: Uses start_new_session or subprocess.Popen pattern for background execution
expectations.evidence: Line 69-70: 'The key to background execution is subprocess.Popen with start_new_session=True.' Lines 84-88: code uses subprocess.Popen(..., start_new_session=True, ...)

expectations.text: Mentions PYTHONUNBUFFERED or python -u for log output
Mentions installing torch, torchvision, wandb dependencies
expectations.evidence: Lines 61-63: 'colab install -s train-cifar torch torchvision wandb' under the heading 'Install Dependencies'

expectations.text: Mentions using relative paths with colab exec -f
expectations.evidence: Line 99: 'colab exec -s train-cifar -f launch_training.py' — uses the relative path 'launch_training.py' with the -f flag

No mention of a 2-4 hour session lifetime. The document mentions a 24-hour keep-alive cap (line 218) and general reclamation on inactivity (line 219), but does not warn about a specific ~2-4 hour session window.

expectations.text: Mentions colab download to retrieve checkpoints
expectations.evidence: Lines 173-178: 'colab download -s train-cifar /content/checkpoints/epoch_10.pt ./checkpoints/epoch_10.pt' under the heading 'Downloading Checkpoints'

expectations.text: Shows how to check on running training (pgrep, log tail, or check_progress script)
expectations.evidence: Lines 131-139: shows pgrep -f train_cifar pattern. Lines 144-151: shows tailing the log. Both under the 'Monitoring Progress' section.
